senses.definition: Resembling but not actually belonging to a particular plant type or species, especially in scientific description.
examples: The fossil showed an allophytoid structure that puzzled the paleobotanists.
Several allophytoid forms were listed in the early survey of the region.

senses.definition: Cytoplasm that comes from a different genetic source than the nucleus, often used in plant breeding and cell biology.
examples: The researchers studied the effect of foreign alloplasm on plant growth.
Alloplasm can influence how nuclear genes are expressed in hybrids.

senses.definition: Relating to or involving alloplasm, especially cytoplasm of foreign genetic origin.
examples: The alloplasmatic line showed increased resistance to disease.
Alloplasmatic changes were observed after the grafting experiment.

senses.definition: Having or involving cytoplasm that comes from a different species or genetic line than the nucleus.
examples: Alloplasmic plants often show altered growth and fertility.
The experiment produced several alloplasmic strains of wheat.

senses.definition: Plural of alloplasm: cytoplasms that have a different genetic origin from the nucleus.
examples: Different alloplasms can produce different visible traits in the same nuclear background.
The study compared several alloplasms in a single plant species.

senses.definition: A synthetic or non‑living material that is used to replace or repair a part of the body in surgery.
examples: The surgeon implanted an alloplast to reconstruct the bone defect.
Modern alloplasts are designed to be safe and long‑lasting.

senses.definition: Made of or relating to alloplasts, especially synthetic materials used to replace body tissue.
examples: Alloplastic implants are common in facial reconstruction.
The dentist chose an alloplastic material for the ridge augmentation.

senses.definition: In psychology, describing behavior that tries to change the outside world rather than adapting oneself.
examples: The patient showed an alloplastic response, blaming others for every problem.
Alloplastic behavior can make therapy more challenging.

senses.definition: Surgery in which a synthetic or non‑living material is used to repair, replace, or shape part of the body.
examples: Alloplasty is often used to correct defects in the jaw or skull.
The team discussed whether alloplasty or bone grafting was more suitable.

senses.definition: The condition of having more than two sets of chromosomes that come from different species.
examples: Alloploidy can create new plant species with useful traits.
The origin of the crop was traced to an ancient event of alloploidy.

senses.definition: An organism that is a hybrid of two different species and has more than two sets of chromosomes from those species.
examples: Bread wheat is an important allopolyploid grown worldwide.
The new species proved to be an allopolyploid formed after hybridization.

senses.definition: Having more than two sets of chromosomes that come from different species.
examples: The allopolyploid genome combines traits from both parent species.
Allopolyploid plants are often more vigorous than their ancestors.

senses.definition: Plural of allopolyploidy: different cases or types of allopolyploidy in organisms.
examples: Various allopolyploidies have been described in flowering plants.
The article reviews known allopolyploidies in crop evolution.

senses.definition: Plural of allopolyploid: organisms that are hybrids of two species and have more than two chromosome sets.
examples: Many crops, such as wheat and cotton, are allopolyploids.
The study compared several allopolyploids with their parent species.

senses.definition: A form of polyploidy in which an organism has three or more sets of chromosomes that come from different species.
examples: Allopolyploidy is a major force in plant speciation.
The textbook explains how allopolyploidy arises after hybridization.

senses.definition: Relating to a person’s mental orientation to the outside world, such as knowing where and when they are.
examples: The doctor checked whether the patient was allopsychic and aware of place and time.
Allopsychic disorientation can appear in some psychiatric conditions.

senses.definition: A drug used to lower uric acid levels in the body, often to treat gout or prevent certain kidney stones.
examples: The doctor prescribed allopurinol for his gout.
Allopurinol helps prevent the formation of uric acid kidney stones.

senses.definition: Different forms, doses, or brands of the drug allopurinol.
examples: The study compared generic allopurinols with the original brand.
Different allopurinols may vary slightly in cost and formulation.

senses.definition: Belonging to a regional or local variety of a language rather than the standard form.
examples: The text includes several alloquial expressions from rural dialects.
Alloquial vocabulary can be hard for outsiders to understand.

senses.definition: A word or expression that belongs to a local or regional variety of a language.
examples: The dictionary lists many alloquialisms from the northern region.
She studied alloquialisms used in everyday rural conversation.

senses.definition: A formal or serious speech addressed to another person or group.
examples: His brief alloquy to the jury was calm and measured.
The judge delivered an alloquy before announcing the sentence.

senses.definition: An irregular or abnormal rhythm, especially of the heartbeat.
examples: The ECG showed signs of allorhythmia during the stress test.
Severe allorhythmia can lead to dizziness and fainting.

senses.definition: Variant spelling of allorhythmia: an abnormal or irregular rhythm, especially of the heart.
examples: Older reports use the term allorrhyhmia for irregular pulse patterns.
Allorrhyhmia was noted only under heavy exercise.

senses.definition: Having or showing an abnormal or irregular rhythm.
examples: Allorrhythmic heart activity was recorded during the night.
The patient’s pulse became allorrhythmic after the medication.

senses.definition: A large meat‑eating dinosaur of the genus Allosaurus that lived in the Jurassic period.
examples: The museum displays a full skeleton of an allosaur.
In the film, a hungry allosaur chases the explorers.

senses.definition: Plural of allosaur: large carnivorous dinosaurs of the genus Allosaurus.
examples: The book explains how allosaurs hunted in packs.
Scientists found evidence of injuries on several allosaurs.

senses.definition: A genus of large meat‑eating dinosaurs that lived in the late Jurassic period.
examples: Allosaurus was one of the top predators of its time.
Children often recognize Allosaurus from dinosaur books.

senses.definition: Plural of allosaurus: large carnivorous dinosaurs of the genus Allosaurus.
examples: The documentary shows how allosauruses may have hunted their prey.
Paleontologists discovered several allosauruses at the site.

senses.definition: A rare sugar that is an isomer of glucose, used mainly in chemical and biochemical research.
examples: Allose is studied for its potential biological effects.
The lab ordered pure allose for the experiment.

senses.definition: Having warning signals that protect another species rather than the one that shows them, in discussions of animal signaling.
examples: Some insects may display allosematic patterns that benefit their partners.
The bright colors were described as allosematic, not self‑protective.

senses.definition: A sex chromosome, such as the X or Y chromosome in humans.
examples: In humans, X and Y chromosomes are allosomes.
Differences in allosomes determine biological sex in many species.

senses.definition: Describing a protein, especially an enzyme, whose activity changes when another molecule binds at a site different from the main active site.
examples: The enzyme has an allosteric site that controls its activity.
Allosteric regulation plays a key role in many metabolic pathways.

senses.definition: In a way that involves binding at an allosteric site and changing a protein’s activity.
examples: The drug acts allosterically to reduce the enzyme’s activity.
The receptor can be activated allosterically by several ligands.

senses.definition: Plural of allostery: different cases or types of allosteric regulation.
examples: The paper reviews various allosteries observed in metabolic enzymes.
Scientists discussed the allosteries of several receptor families.

senses.definition: The process by which a molecule binding at one site on a protein changes the activity at another site.
examples: Allostery allows enzymes to respond quickly to changes in the cell.
Understanding allostery can help design better drugs.

senses.definition: Pairing of non‑identical but related chromosomes during meiosis, often in hybrids or polyploids.
examples: Allosyndesis can lead to new gene combinations in hybrids.
The cytologist observed frequent allosyndesis in the polyploid plants.

senses.definition: Showing or involving allosyndesis, especially in the pairing of chromosomes.
examples: Allosyndetic pairing was common in the hybrid generation.
The species forms allosyndetic bivalents during meiosis.

senses.definition: To give a share of something to each person or purpose, often as part of a plan.
examples: The manager allotted a separate desk to everyone.
Each student was allotted ten minutes for the presentation.

senses.definition: To set something aside for a special use or purpose.
examples: We try to allot one evening a week to family activities.
They allotted part of the budget to staff training.

senses.definition: Non‑standard or misspelled form of allotted; used to describe something that has been given or assigned.
examples: Each speaker was alloted five minutes in the program.
The alloted funds were not enough to finish the project.

senses.definition: A person or organization that receives land, shares, or another resource that has been officially allotted.
examples: Each allotee must register the land within six months.
The company informed every allotee about the share issue.

senses.definition: Caused by or relating to electrical currents that come from outside the Earth.
examples: The study focused on allotelluric currents affecting the measurements.
Allotelluric influences were considered in the geophysical model.

senses.definition: An organism with four sets of chromosomes that come from two different species.
examples: The new allotetraploid combined traits of both parent species.
Several crop plants are natural allotetraploids.

senses.definition: Plural of allotetraploidy: different cases where organisms have four chromosome sets from two species.
examples: Repeated allotetraploidies have shaped the evolution of this plant group.
The review article summarizes known allotetraploidies in legumes.

senses.definition: Plural of allotetraploid: organisms with four sets of chromosomes from two different species.
examples: Several allotetraploids were produced in the breeding program.
Wild allotetraploids may show high genetic diversity.

senses.definition: The condition of having four chromosome sets that come from two different species.
examples: Allotetraploidy often results from hybridization followed by chromosome doubling.
The species’ allotetraploidy is evident from its genome structure.

senses.definition: Belief in or worship of a god that is different from the accepted or traditional god of one’s own group.
examples: The preacher warned against allotheism and foreign cults.
Historical records show periods of tension over suspected allotheism.

senses.definition: Plural of allotheism: different forms of belief in other or foreign gods.
examples: The text lists several allotheisms that appeared in the region.
Rulers often tried to suppress new allotheisms.

senses.definition: A person who believes in or worships a different god from the one accepted by their own group.
examples: He was branded an allotheist by the strict community leaders.
Allotheists were sometimes forced to leave the city.

senses.definition: Relating to or supporting the worship of a god that is different from the accepted god of a group.
examples: Allotheistic practices were forbidden by royal decree.
The poem contains allotheistic images drawn from foreign myths.

senses.definition: A group of extinct mammals that includes multituberculates, known mainly from fossils.
examples: Allotheria were small mammals that lived alongside dinosaurs.
The jaw structure of Allotheria is quite distinctive.

senses.definition: Originating in a different place, especially describing rocks or sediments that came from outside the area where they are found.
examples: The allothigene blocks were carried into the basin by landslides.
Allothigene material indicates long‑distance transport.

senses.definition: Formed from material that came from another place, especially in geology or sedimentology.
examples: The allothigenetic deposits contrast with the locally derived layers.
Allothigenetic sediments suggest strong ancient currents.
